question: Let a population have mean μ = 50 and variance σ² = 100. For samples of size n = 25, the variance of the sampling distribution of the mean is:
Which statement about Central Limit Theorem is FALSE?
options: It applies to the sample mean

question: If P = 0.6 and n = 150, what is the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of p̂?
A sample of size 36 has mean 80 and population standard deviation 12. The margin of error for a 95% CI is:
The width of a confidence interval for the population mean will decrease when:
A 95% CI for a mean is (46.5, 51.5). Which statement is correct?
options: The population mean has a 95% probability of lying in the interval
95% of sample means lie in this interval
The CI is constructed so that 95% of such intervals contain μ
The confidence level equals the sample mean
correct_answer: The CI is constructed so that 95% of such intervals contain μ

question: When σ² is unknown and sample size is small, the appropriate distribution for CI estimation of μ is:
A variable is normally distributed with mean μ = 50 and standard deviation σ = 10. What is the Z-score for a value of 65?
A sample of size 16 is drawn from a normal population. The chi-square statistic for variance estimation has degrees of freedom equal to:
